name: guizero

metadata.name: guizero
metadata.summary: Python module to allow learners to easily create GUIs

documentation:
# guizero

guizero is designed to allow new learners to quickly and easily create GUIs for their programs.

There is comprehensive documentation at [lawsie.github.io/guizero](https://lawsie.github.io/guizero/)

## Install

If you can download and unzip a file, you can [install guizero](https://lawsie.github.io/guizero/#easy-install) - **no special permissions or administrator rights are required**.

If you have administrator rights and are connected to the internet, you can [use the command line to install or upgrade guizero](https://lawsie.github.io/guizero/#install-using-command-line).

guizero only requires `tkinter` to be installed, which is included with a standard Python installation on all platforms except Linux.
A python module to allow learners to easily create GUIs. guizero is designed to be used by new learners.

## Use

guizero is simple to use, taking away much of the complexity of creating simple GUIs.

```python
from guizero import App, Text
app = App(title="Hello world")
message = Text(app, text="Welcome to the Hello world app!")
app.display()
```

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Drawing

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the Drawing will belong too.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the container, defaults to None.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.line
documentation:
Draws a line between 2 points

:param int x1:
    The x position of the starting point.

:param int y1:
    The y position of the starting point.

:param int x2:
    The x position of the end point.

:param int y2:
    The y position of the end point.

:param str color:
    The color of the line. Defaults to `"black"`.

:param int width:
    The width of the line. Defaults to `1`.

:return:
    The id of the line.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.oval
documentation:
Draws an oval between 2 points

:param int x1:
    The x position of the starting point.

:param int y1:
    The y position of the starting point.

:param int x2:
    The x position of the end point.

:param int y2:
    The y position of the end point.

:param str color:
    The color of the shape. Defaults to `"black"`.

:param int outline:
    `0` or `False` is no outline. `True` or value > 1 sets an outline. Defaults to `False`.

:param str outline_color:
    The color of the outline. Defaults to `"black"`.

:return:
    The id of the shape.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.rectangle
documentation:
Draws a rectangle between 2 points

:param int x1:
    The x position of the starting point.

:param int y1:
    The y position of the starting point.

:param int x2:
    The x position of the end point.

:param int y2:
    The y position of the end point.

:param str color:
    The color of the shape. Defaults to `"black"`.

:param int outline:
    `0` or `False` is no outline. `True` or value > 1 sets an outline. Defaults to `False`.

:param str outline_color:
    The color of the outline. Defaults to `"black"`.

:return:
    The id of the shape.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.polygon
documentation:
Draws a polygon from an list of co-ordinates

:param int *coords:
    Pairs of x and y positions which make up the polygon.

:param str color:
    The color of the shape. Defaults to `"black"`.

:param int outline:
    `0` or `False` is no outline. `True` or value > 1 sets an outline. Defaults to `False`.

:param str outline_color:
    The color of the outline. Defaults to `"black"`.

:return:
    The id of the shape.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.triangle
documentation:
Draws a triangle between 3 points

:param int x1:
    The x position of the starting point.

:param int y1:
    The y position of the starting point.

:param int x2:
    The x position of the middle point.

:param int y2:
    The y position of the middle point.

:param int x3:
    The x position of the end point.

:param int y3:
    The y position of the end point.

:param str color:
    The color of the shape. Defaults to `"black"`.

:param int outline:
    `0` or `False` is no outline. `True` or value > 1 sets an outline. Defaults to `False`.

:param str outline_color:
    The color of the outline. Defaults to `"black"`.

:return:
    The id of the shape.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.image
documentation:
Inserts an image into the drawing, position by its top-left corner.

:param int x:
    The x position to insert the image.

:param int y:
    The y position to insert the image.

:param str image:
    The file path or a PhotoImage or PIL.Image object.

:param str width:
    The width to scale the image too, setting to `None` will use the
    actual width of the Image. Default to `None`.

:param str height:
    The width to scale the image too, setting to `None` will use the 
    actual height of the Image. Default to `None`.

:return:
    The id of the image.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.text
documentation:
Inserts text into the drawing, position by its top-left corner.

:param int x:
    The x position of the text.

:param int y:
    The x position of the text.

:param str color:
    The color of the text. Defaults to `"black"`.

:param str font:
    The font to use. Defaults to `None` and will use the system
    default font.

:param int size:
    The size of the text. Defaults to `None` and will use the system
    default font size.

:param int max_width:
    Maximum line length. Lines longer than this value are wrapped. 
    Default is `None` (no wrapping).

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.delete
documentation:
Deletes an "object" (line, triangle, image, etc) from the drawing.

:param int id:
    The id of the object.

methods.name: guizero.Drawing.Drawing.clear
documentation: Clears the drawing.

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the CheckBox will belong too.

:param string selected:
    The text required for the checkbox. Defaults to "".

:param Callable command:
    The callback function to call when the CheckBox changes.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to None.

:param callback args:
    A list of arguments to pass to the widgets `command`, defaults to
    `None`.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

methods.name: guizero.ButtonGroup.ButtonGroup.__init__
documentation:
Creates a ButtonGroup

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the ButtonGroup will belong too.

:param List option:
    A list of options to append to the ButtonGroup. If a 2D list is
    specified, the first element is the text, the second is the value,
    defaults to an empty list.

:param string selected:
    The item in the ButtonGroup to select, defaults to `None`.

:param string horizontal:
    If the ButtonGroup is to be displayed horizontally, defaults to
    `True`.

:param Callable command:
    The callback function to call when the ButtonGroup changes,
    defaults to `None`.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to None.

:param callback args:
    A list of arguments to pass to the widgets `command`, defaults to
    `None`.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

functions.name: guizero.dialog.warn
documentation:
Display a warning message box.

:param string title:
    The title to be displayed on the box.
:param string text:

:param App master:
    Optional guizero master which the popup will be placed over. Defaults
    to `None`.
:return:
    None.

functions.name: guizero.dialog.yesno
documentation:
Alert type yes or no

:param string title:
    The title to be displayed on the box.
:param string text:
    The text to be displayed on the box.
:param App master:
    Optional guizero master which the popup will be placed over. Defaults
    to `None`.
:return:
    True if 'yes', False if 'no'

functions.name: guizero.dialog.question
documentation:
Display a question box which can accept a text response.

If Ok is pressed the value entered into the box is returned.

If Cancel is pressed `""` is returned.

:param string title:
    The title to be displayed on the box.
:param string text:
    The text to be displayed on the box.
:param string initial_value:
    The default value for the response box.
:param App master:
    Optional guizero master which the popup will be placed over. Defaults
    to `None`.
:return:
    The value entered or `None`.

functions.name: guizero.dialog.select_file
documentation:
Display a box to select a file to open or save.

If Open or Save is pressed the filename path is returned.

If Cancel is pressed `None` is returned.

```
param string title

The title to be displayed on the box. Defaults to 'Select file'.

param string folder

The initial folder to open. Defaults to '.'.

param list filetypes

A list of file descriptions and wildcards of file types to display.
Provide as a list of lists e.g.

source:14: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

filetypes=[["Text documents", "*.txt"], ["CSV files", "*.csv"]]

param boolean save

The default value for the response box.

param App master

Optional guizero master which the popup will be placed over. Defaults
to .

return

The path of filename selected or .
```

functions.name: guizero.dialog.select_folder
documentation:
Display a box to select a folder.

If a folder is selected the folder path is returned, otherwise `""` is returned.

:param string title:
    The title to be displayed on the box. Defaults to 'Select file'.
:param string folder:
    The initial folder to open. Defaults to '.'.
:param App master:
    Optional guizero master which the popup will be placed over. Defaults
    to `None`.
:return:
    The path of folder selected or `None`.

functions.name: guizero.dialog.select_color
documentation:
Display a box to select a color.

```
param color

Preselected color. Defaults to .

param App master

Optional guizero master which the popup will be placed over. Defaults
to .

return

The color in code #rrggbb or .
```

methods.name: guizero.MenuBar.MenuBar.__init__
documentation:
Creates a MenuBar

```
param Container master

The Container (App, Box, etc) the MenuBar will belong too.

param List toplevel

A list of strings to populate the top level menu options.

param List options

A 3D list of:

submenus,

with each submenu being a list of options

and each option being a text/command pair

e.g

options=[
        [ ["File option 1", file_function], ["File option 2", file_function] ],
        [ ["Edit option 1", edit_function], ["Edit option 2", edit_function] ]
    ]
```

methods.name: guizero.MenuBar.MenuBar.bg
documentation:
Sets the background color of the widget.

Note - some operating systems dont allow the background color of the 
menu bar to be changed.

methods.name: guizero.Combo.Combo.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Combo

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the Combo will belong too.

:param List option:
    A list of strings to populate the Combo, defaults to an empty list.

:param string selected:
    The item in the Combo to select, defaults to `None`.

:param Callable command:
    The callback function to call when the Combo changes,
    defaults to `None`.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to None.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

methods.name: guizero.Combo.Combo.value

methods.name: guizero.Combo.Combo.update_command
documentation:
Updates the callback command which is called when the Combo
changes.

Setting to `None` stops the callback.

:param Callable command:
    The callback function to call, it can accept 0 or 1 parameters.

    If it accepts 1 parameter the `value` of the Combo will be
    passed.

methods.name: guizero.Box.Box.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Box

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the Box will belong too.

:param string layout:
    The layout the Box should use "auto" or "grid. Defaults to "auto".

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to `None`.

:param callback args:
    A list of arguments to pass to the widgets `command`, defaults to
    `None`.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size. If not `None`, both the width and height need to be set.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size. If not `None`, both the width and height need to be set.

:param int border:
    Sets the border thickness. `0` or `False` is no border. `True` or 
    value > 1 sets a border. The default is `None`.

methods.name: guizero.Box.Box.border
documentation:
Sets or returns the border thickness.

`0` or `False` is no border.
`True` or value > 1 sets a border

documentation: Returns the current platform ("linux", "darwin", "win32")

methods.name: guizero.utilities.SystemConfig.tk_options
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of tk options in the format {"pattern": value}
which will be applied when an App is created.

The tk options can be used to modify the default behaviour of
tk and its widgets e.g. Change the background colour of all Buttons

```
from guizero import system_config
system_config.tk_options["*Button.Background"] = "green"
```
holds details about the configuration guizero is using

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.__init__
documentation:
GUIZeroImage manages an "image" for guizero widgets, parsing its
contents, sizing it accordingly and managing environment.

:param string image_source:
    The source of the image, a file path, PIL or
    Tk image object.

:param int width:
    The required width of the image, set to `None`, to keep the
    original image width

:param int height:
    The required height of the image, set to `None`, to keep the
    original image width.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.image_source
documentation:
Returns the original source of the image, be that a file path, PIL or
Tk image object.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.tk_image
documentation: Returns the Tk PhotoImage object.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.pil_image
documentation: Returns the PIL Image object.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.width
documentation: Returns the image width.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.height
documentation: Returns the image height.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.animation
documentation:
Returns `True` if the image contains more than 1 frame (i.e. is an
animation)

methods.name: guizero.utilities.GUIZeroImage.tk_frames
documentation:
Returns a list of frames as Tk PhotoImage objects which make up this
image.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.AnimationPlayer.__init__
documentation:
AnimationPlayer manages the playing of a animated gif for guizero
widgets.

:param Widget master:
    The widget which is displaying the animation.

:param GUIZeroImage guizero_image:
    The image object which contains the animation.

:param Callable update_image_callback:
    A function which should be called when the Image needs updating.
    The function will be called and passed a reference to the next
    Tk PhotoImage object in the animation.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.AnimationPlayer.running
documentation: Returns `True` if the animation is running

methods.name: guizero.utilities.AnimationPlayer.start
documentation: Starts the animation.

methods.name: guizero.utilities.AnimationPlayer.stop
documentation: Stops the animation

methods.name: guizero.utilities.TriggeredList.__init__
documentation:
A list which will call a callback when a value is changed.

Used to hold a widgets grid reference.

methods.name: guizero.TitleBox.TitleBox.__init__
documentation:
Creates a TitleBox

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the TitleBox will belong too.

:param string text:
    The text to be displayed on the box title.

:param string layout:
    The layout the TitleBox should use "auto" or "grid. Defaults to "auto".

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to `None`.

:param callback args:
    A list of arguments to pass to the widgets `command`, defaults to
    `None`.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size. If not `None`, both the width and height need to be set.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size. If not `None`, both the width and height need to be set.

:param int border:
    Sets the border thickness. `0` or `False` is no border. `True` or
    value > 1 sets a border. The default is `2`.

methods.name: guizero.TitleBox.TitleBox.border
documentation:
Sets or returns the border thickness.

`0` or `False` is no border.
`True` or value > 1 sets a border

methods.name: guizero.TitleBox.TitleBox.text
documentation: Sets of returns the text used in the title

methods.name: guizero.TitleBox.TitleBox.resize
documentation:
Resizes the widget.

:param int width:
    The width of the widget.

:param int height:
    The height of the widget.

Internal use only

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle._size_waffle

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.set_all

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.set_pixel
documentation: Sets a single pixel

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.get_pixel
documentation: Returns the colour value of a pixel if set

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.get_all
documentation: Returns a 2D list of all colours in the waffle

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle._clicked_on
documentation: Internal use only
Detect x,y coords of where the user clicked

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.pixel

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.disable

documentation: Enable the widget.

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.bg

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.bg
documentation: Set the background colour

methods.name: guizero.Waffle.Waffle.description
documentation: Returns the description for the widget.

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.__init__
documentation:
Creates a ListBox

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the ListBox will belong too.

:param List items:
    A list of strings to populate the ListBox, defaults to `None`.

:param string selected:
    The item in the ListBox to select, defaults to `None`.

:param Callable command:
    The callback function to call when the ListBox changes,
    defaults to `None`.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to None.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param bool multiselect:
    If ListBox should allow multiple items to be selected, defaults
    to `False`.

:param bool scrollbar:
    If ListBox should have a vertical scrollbar, defaults to False.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.resize
documentation:
Resizes the widget.

:param int width:
    The width of the widget.

:param int height:
    The height of the widget.

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.value
documentation:
Sets or returns the items selected in a ListBox

`None` if 0 items are selected.

If the ListBox is a not `multiselect`, `value` is the item selected.

If the ListBox is a `multiselect`, `value` is a list of items
selected.

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.insert
documentation: Insert a new `item` at `index`

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.append
documentation: Appends a new `item` to the end of the ListBox.

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.remove
documentation:
Removes the first `item` from the ListBox.

Returns `True` if an item was removed.

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.clear
documentation: Clears all the items in a ListBox

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.items
documentation: Returns a list of items in the ListBox

methods.name: guizero.ListBox.ListBox.update_command
documentation:
Updates the callback command which is called when the ListBox
changes.

Setting to `None` stops the callback.

:param Callable command:
    The callback function to call, it can accept 0 or 1 parameters.

    If it accepts 1 parameter the `value` of the ListBox will be
    passed.

methods.name: guizero.PushButton.PushButton.__init__
documentation:
Creates a PushButton

:param Container master:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the Picture will belong to.

:param Callable command:
    A string containing the image to display, defaults to `None`.

:param List args:
    A list of arguments to pass to the command. Defaults to `None`.

:param string text:
    The text to display on the button, defaults to `Button`.

:param string image:
    A string containing the image to display, defaults to `None`.
    If an image is specified, this overrides any text set to display
    on the button.

:param int padx:
    The amount of horizontal padding the button should have. Defaults to 10.

:param int pady:
    The amount of vertical padding the button should have. Defaults to 10.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to None.

:param string icon:
    A string containing the image to display, defaults to `None`.
    If an image is specified, this overrides any text set to display
    on the button. (DEPRECATED)

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

methods.name: guizero.PushButton.PushButton.value
documentation: PROPERTIES
Get the value of the button (pressed, released)

methods.name: guizero.PushButton.PushButton.text

documentation: Set the text on the button

Internal use only
Called when the button is pressed

documentation: Change padding

The text value

Clear text (set to empty string)

methods.name: guizero.Text.Text.append
documentation: Append some text onto the end of the existing text

methods.name: guizero.Window.Window.show

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.__init__
documentation:
The EventData class represents a single event and is passed back to
the callback

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.widget
documentation: The guizero widget which raised the event

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.tk_event
documentation: The tkinter event which was returned by the event

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.key
documentation: The key which was pressed and generated the event.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.keycode
documentation: The keycode of the key which was pressed and generated the event.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.x
documentation:
The x position of the mouse relative to the upper left hand corner of
the widget when the event occurred.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.y
documentation:
The y position of the mouse relative to the upper left hand corner of
the widget when the event occurred.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.display_x
documentation:
The x position of the mouse relative to the upper left hand corner of
the display when the event occurred.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.display_y
documentation:
The y position of the mouse relative to the upper left hand corner of
the display when the event occurred.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.width
documentation: The new width of the widget when re-configured.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventData.height
documentation: The new height of the widget when re-configured.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventCallback.__init__
documentation:
The EventCallback handles all the callbacks for a single tk event
(e.g. <Button-1>) on a guizero widget.

By using the EventCallback structure you can assign multiple callbacks
to 1 tk event across multiple tk widgets.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventCallback.get_callback
documentation: Returns the callback for a ref (reference)

methods.name: guizero.event.EventCallback.set_callback
documentation: Sets a callback for a ref (reference), setting to None will remove it

methods.name: guizero.event.EventCallback.remove_callback
documentation: Removes a callback for a ref (reference),

methods.name: guizero.event.EventCallback.rebind
documentation:
Rebinds the tk event, only used if a widget has been destroyed
and recreated.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventManager.__init__
documentation:
The EventManager handles all the events and callbacks for a guizero
widget.

Every event created must be given a reference, this reference
is how events are managed internally within guizero.

A guizero widget can contain many tk widgets, so all the tk objects
for this guizero widget need to passed.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventManager.get_event
documentation: Returns the event callback for a ref (reference)

methods.name: guizero.event.EventManager.set_event
documentation:
Sets a callback for this widget against a ref (reference) for a
tk_event, setting the callback to None will remove it.

methods.name: guizero.event.EventManager.remove_event
documentation: Removes an event for a ref (reference),

methods.name: guizero.event.EventManager.rebind_events
documentation:
Rebinds all the tk events, only used if a tk widget has been destroyed
and recreated.

methods.name: guizero.App.App.__init__
documentation:
Creates an App

:param string title:
    The text in the title bar of the window, defaults to `guizero`.

:param int width:
    The width of the App window, defaults to 500.

:param int height:
    The height of the App window, defaults to 500.

:param string layout:
    The layout manager style for this window, defaults to `auto`.

:param color bg:
    The background colour for this window, defaults to None. See https://lawsie.github.io/guizero/colors/

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

methods.name: guizero.App.App.display
documentation:
Display the window.

:return:
    None.
METHODS

methods.name: guizero.App.App.destroy
documentation:
Destroy and close the App.

:return:
    None.

 :note:
    Once destroyed an App can no longer be used.

documentation: Repeat `function` every `time` milliseconds.

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.ScheduleMixin.cancel
documentation: Cancel the scheduled `function` calls.

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.ScheduleMixin._call_wrapper
documentation: Fired by tk.after, gets the callback and either executes the function and cancels or repeats

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.DestroyMixin.destroy

documentation: Show the widget.

documentation: these static methods are used where classes need to inherit 'text' properties
from multiple classes and obvs cant, e.g. ContainerTextWidget

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.TextMixin.text_color
documentation: Sets or returns the text color of the widget.
Get the text colour as a string

documentation: Set the text colour

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.TextMixin.font

Get the current font as a string

Get the current text size as an integer

documentation: Set the font size

documentation: Sets the background color of the widget.
Get the background colour as a string

documentation: Set the background colour

Resizes the widget.

:param int height:
    The height of the widget.

documentation: Sets or returns `[x,y]` coordinates of this widget.

Validates a Widgets grid property and stores it as a TriggeredList
which will call the masters display_widgets method when it is changed

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.LayoutMixin.align

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.LayoutMixin._update_align
documentation: Validates a widgets align property

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_clicked

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_double_clicked

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_left_button_pressed

Sets or returns the function called when the left mouse button is
pressed.

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_left_button_released

Sets or returns the function called when the left mouse button is
released.

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_right_button_pressed

Sets or returns the function called when the right mouse button is
pressed.

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_right_button_released

Sets or returns the function called when the right mouse button is
released.

Sets or returns the function called when the mouse pointer enters

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_mouse_leaves
documentation:
Sets or returns the function called when the mouse pointer leaves
the widget.

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_mouse_moved

methods.name: guizero.tkmixins.EventsMixin.when_mouse_dragged
documentation:
Sets or returns the function called when the mouse pointer is dragged
(moved with the left button pressed).

modules.name: guizero.base
documentation: Abstract classes for guizero.

methods.name: guizero.base.Base.__init__
documentation:
Base class for all components in guizero.

```
param tk

Top level widget instance of Tkinter which usually is the main window of the application

return

None.
```

methods.name: guizero.base.Base.tk
documentation:
Returns the tk widget.

:return:
    tk widget

methods.name: guizero.base.Base._has_tk_config
documentation:
Checks whether the key is configured or not.

```
param key

Key

return

Keys in tk.keys
```

methods.name: guizero.base.Base._get_tk_config
documentation:
Gets the config from the widget's tk object.

:param string key:
    The tk config key.

:param bool default:
    Returns the default value for this key. Defaults to `False`.

methods.name: guizero.base.Base._set_tk_config
documentation:
Gets the config from the widget's tk object

:param string/List keys:
    The tk config key or a list of tk keys.

:param variable value:
    The value to set. If the value is `None`, the config value will be
    reset to its default.

methods.name: guizero.base.Component.__init__
documentation: An abstract class for a component in guizero.

methods.name: guizero.base.Component.master
documentation:
Returns the master (container) of this widget, or `None` if it doesn't have one.

:return:
    Master container of widget, 'None' if it doesn't exists.

methods.name: guizero.base.Component.description
documentation: Returns the description for the widget.

methods.name: guizero.base.Component.events
documentation: Returns the EventManager which can be used to set custom event handlers.

methods.name: guizero.base.Component.displayable
documentation:
Returns whether the Component can be displayed (packed or gridded)

Note: this is only used by MenuBar and is a candidate for refactoring

methods.name: guizero.base.Component.destroy
documentation: Destroy the tk widget.

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.__init__
documentation: An abstract class for a container which can hold other widgets.

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.layout
documentation: Returns the layout type used by this container.

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.bg
documentation: Sets or returns the background color of the container.

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.text_color
documentation:
Sets and returns the text color to be used by the widgets
in the container.

If set to None (the default) any widgets added to this container
will use the default text color

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.text_size
documentation:
Sets and returns the text size to be used by the widgets
in the container.

If set to None (the default) any widgets added to this container
will use the default text size

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.font
documentation:
Sets and returns the font to be used by the widgets
in the container.

If set to None (the default) any widgets added to this container
will use the default font

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.children
documentation: Returns a list of children widgets

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.add_tk_widget
documentation:
Adds a tk widget into a guizero container.

:param tkinter.Widget tk_widget:
    The Container (App, Box, etc) the tk widget will belong too.

:param List grid:
    Grid co-ordinates for the widget, required if the master layout
    is 'grid', defaults to `None`.

:param string align:
    How to align the widget within the grid, defaults to None.

:param bool visible:
    If the widget should be visible, defaults to `True`.

:param bool enabled:
    If the widget should be enabled, defaults to `None`. If `None`
    the value is inherited from the master.

:param int width:
    The starting width of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

:param int height:
    The starting height of the widget. Defaults to `None` and will auto
    size.

methods.name: guizero.base.Container._add_child
documentation:
Associates a child widget with this widget.

Child widgets are used to cascaded to properties (e.g. bg)

methods.name: guizero.base.Container._remove_child
documentation: Removes a child widgets association with this widget.

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.display_widgets
documentation:
Displays all the widgets associated with this Container.

Should be called when the widgets need to be "re-packed/gridded".

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.enabled
documentation:
Sets or Returns the enabled status of this container.

Setting the property will change the enabled status of all
widgets in this container

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.disable
documentation: Disable all the widgets in this container

methods.name: guizero.base.Container.enable
documentation: Enable all the widgets in this container

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.__init__
documentation: Base class for objects which use windows e.g. `App` and `Window`

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.title
documentation: Sets or returns the title displayed in the title bar.
PROPERTIES
The title text

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.height
documentation: Sets or returns the height of the window
The height of the window

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.width
documentation: Sets or returns the width of the window
The width of the window

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.visible
documentation: Sets or returns the visibility of the window

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.full_screen
documentation: Sets or returns the visibility of the window
Whether the window is full screen or not

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.resize
documentation:
Resizes the window.

:param int width:
    The width of the window.

:param int height:
    The height of the window.
METHODS

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.hide
documentation: Hide the window.

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.show
documentation: Show the window.

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.set_full_screen
documentation: Make this window full screen and bind the Escape key (or given key) to exit full screen mode

methods.name: guizero.base.BaseWindow.exit_full_screen
documentation: Change from full screen to windowed mode and remove key binding

methods.name: guizero.base.Widget.__init__
documentation: The base class for a widget which is an interactable component e.g. `Picture`

methods.name: guizero.base.TextWidget.__init__
documentation: The base class for a widget which contains or has text e.g. ``Text`, `PushButton`

methods.name: guizero.base.ContainerWidget.__init__
documentation: The base class for a widget which is also a container e.g. `Box`, `ButtonGroup`

methods.name: guizero.base.ContainerWidget._set_propagation
documentation:
Set the propagation value of the tk widget dependent on the width and height

:param int width:
    The width of the widget.

:param int height:
    The height of the widget.

methods.name: guizero.base.ContainerTextWidget.__init__
documentation:
The base class for a widget which is also a container and contains text
e.g. `TitleBox`

methods.name: guizero.base.ContainerTextWidget.text_color
documentation: Sets or returns the text color of the widget.

methods.name: guizero.base.ContainerTextWidget.font
documentation: Set or returns the font the widget is using.

methods.name: guizero.base.ContainerTextWidget.text_size
documentation: Sets or returns the text size of the widget.
